  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c-drl", "name": "data readiness levels", "aliases": ["DRL", "data readiness bands"], "description": "A proposed scale (bands A, B, C with sub-levels) for characterizing how ready a data set is for analysis.", "grounding_span": "we introduce the idea of data readiness levels", "location": "2 Data Readiness Levels"},
    {"id": "c-formula", "name": "data + model -> prediction", "aliases": ["universal formula of machine learning"], "description": "The stated universal formula of machine learning, where prediction quality depends on both model and data.", "grounding_span": "data + model → prediction", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "c-dataquality", "name": "data quality", "aliases": [], "description": "The quality of data as a determinant of prediction quality, argued to be under-controlled relative to model quality.", "grounding_span": "control over the quality of our data", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "c-ml", "name": "machine learning", "aliases": [], "description": "The modeling discipline whose academic focus, per the paper, over-emphasizes model quality over data quality.", "grounding_span": "through machine learning", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c-bandc", "name": "Band C", "aliases": ["C band", "accessibility band"], "description": "The band concerned with the accessibility of a data set, from hearsay (C4) to loadable (C1).", "grounding_span": "Band C is about the accessibility of a data set.", "location": "2.1 Band C"},
    {"id": "c-bandb", "name": "Band B", "aliases": ["B band", "faithfulness band"], "description": "The band concerned with the faithfulness and representation of the data once loaded.", "grounding_span": "Band B is about the faithfulness and representation of the da ta.", "location": "2.2 Band B"},
    {"id": "c-banda", "name": "Band A", "aliases": ["A band", "data in context"], "description": "The band concerned with a data set's appropriateness for a defined task or question.", "grounding_span": "Band A is about data in context.", "location": "2.3 Band A"},
    {"id": "c-hearsay", "name": "hearsay data", "aliases": ["C4"], "description": "Data believed to exist but whose existence is unverified; the lowest sub-level of Band C.", "grounding_span": "We might think of it as hearsay data.", "location": "2.1 Band C"},
    {"id": "c-c1", "name": "C1 readiness", "aliases": ["C1"], "description": "Data that is machine readable, ethically handled, and ready to be loaded into analysis software.", "grounding_span": "When data arrives at C1", "location": "2.1 Band C"},
    {"id": "c-b1", "name": "B1 readiness", "aliases": ["B1"], "description": "The end of Band B, at which the analyst knows how faithful the data is to its description.", "grounding_span": "By the end of Band B, when data is B1", "location": "2.2 Band B"},
    {"id": "c-a1", "name": "A1 readiness", "aliases": ["A1"], "description": "Data ready to be deployed in a given context; can be A1 for one question but only B1 for another.", "grounding_span": "to be A1 for one question", "location": "2.3 Band A"},
    {"id": "c-trl", "name": "technology readiness levels", "aliases": ["TRL"], "description": "The NASA numbered readiness scale that serves as the analogue and inspiration for data readiness levels.", "grounding_span": "Technology readiness levels arose in NASA", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "c-wrangling", "name": "data munging / data wrangling", "aliases": ["data munging", "data wrangling"], "description": "The laborious accessibility work of Band C, though the paper notes ethical and legal work is also part of the band.", "grounding_span": "data wrangling", "location": "2.1 Band C"},
    {"id": "c-eda", "name": "Exploratory Data Analysis", "aliases": ["EDA"], "description": "Tukey's approach of visualizing data to render it vivid, placed within Band B.", "grounding_span": "Exploratory Data Analysis", "location": "2.2 Band B"},
    {"id": "c-vivid", "name": "vivid information", "aliases": ["pallid information", "non-vivid information"], "description": "Information weighted more heavily in reasoning than pallid information; data readiness aims to render data vivid.", "grounding_span": "the idea of vivid information", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "c-availability", "name": "availability heuristics", "aliases": [], "description": "The cognitive bias the paper relates to why data must be rendered vivid to be accounted for in planning.", "grounding_span": "This may relate to a vailability", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "c-missingvalues", "name": "missing values", "aliases": [], "description": "A representational hazard examined in Band B (encoding, agglomeration before handling).", "grounding_span": "missing values handled", "location": "2.2 Band B"},
    {"id": "c-pipeline", "name": "the analysis pipeline", "aliases": [], "description": "The connected sequence of readiness processes, each requiring different skill sets but not fully separable.", "grounding_span": "The Analysis Pipeline", "location": "3 The Analysis Pipeline"},
    {"id": "c-readinessstage", "name": "readiness stage", "aliases": [], "description": "The idea, drawn from technology deployment, of characterizing readiness to render something vivid.", "grounding_span": "its readiness stage", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "c-metadatascience", "name": "meta data science", "aliases": [], "description": "The study of data science itself through acquiring data about data science.", "grounding_span": "the study of data science itself", "location": "4 Case Studies"},
    {"id": "c-taskcontext", "name": "task/context definition", "aliases": ["context definition"], "description": "The precondition for Band A: a data set can only be considered in Band A once a task is defined.", "grounding_span": "once a task is deﬁned", "location": "2.3 Band A"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d-vivid", "term": "vivid information", "verbatim_text": "information that is weighted more heavily in reasoning than non-vivid or pallid information", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2017", "grounding_span": "information that is weighted more heavily in reasoning than non-vivid or pallid information", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "d-hearsay", "term": "hearsay data", "verbatim_text": "that you’ve heard about so you say it’s there.", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2017", "grounding_span": "that you’ve heard about so you say it’s there.", "location": "2.1 Band C"},
    {"id": "d-bandc", "term": "Band C", "verbatim_text": "Band C is about the accessibility of a data set.", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2017", "grounding_span": "Band C is about the accessibility of a data set.", "location": "2.1 Band C"},
    {"id": "d-bandb", "term": "Band B", "verbatim_text": "Band B is about the faithfulness and representation of the da ta.", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2017", "grounding_span": "Band B is about the faithfulness and representation of the da ta.", "location": "2.2 Band B"},
    {"id": "d-banda", "term": "Band A", "verbatim_text": "Band A is about data in context.", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2017", "grounding_span": "Band A is about data in context.", "location": "2.3 Band A"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl-quality-dependent", "claim_text": "High quality of prediction depends on both good models and high quality data.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "a high quality of prediction being dependent on both", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl-underresourced", "claim_text": "In practice the process of improving data quality is underappreciated and under-resourced.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "the process of improving data quality is under", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl-b1-expensive", "claim_text": "Reaching B1 is often the most expensive part of the project, yet there are no good methods to gauge progress.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "often the most expensive part of the project", "location": "2.2 Band B"},
    {"id": "cl-context-dependent", "claim_text": "A data set can be A1 for one question but only B1 for a different question, so context definition is a prerequisite.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "it is possible for a data s et to be A1 for one question", "location": "2.3 Band A"},
    {"id": "cl-curation-underestimated", "claim_text": "Costs of data curation are often underestimated and those doing Band C and B work are undervalued.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "are often underestimated", "location": "5 Conclusion"},
    {"id": "cl-bandb-error-banda", "claim_text": "A data processing error at Band B (aggregation over missing values) produced a negative result at Band A.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "due to a data processing error at Band B", "location": "4.3 Disease Monitoring in Uganda"},
    {"id": "cl-trl-nasa", "claim_text": "Technology readiness levels arose in NASA.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Technology readiness levels arose in NASA", "location": "1 Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl-collation-dominated", "claim_text": "Data collation took roughly 70-80% of the work on the Uganda malaria project.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "data collation in itself took probably around 70-80%", "location": "4.3 Disease Monitoring in Uganda"},
    {"id": "cl-pmlr-papers", "claim_text": "The PMLR proceedings contain over 3,198 papers.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "They contain over 3,198 papers.", "location": "4.1 Proceedings of Machine Learning Research"}
  ],
